Undergraduate & graduate totals

Aggregated metrics for all programs at each degree level (including Sankey inflow and funnel); landing pages are the degree-level hubs on uagc.edu. Enrollment migration and inquiry-vs-applied flow are not shown at this level (see per-program detail pages).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage). Segment and funnel volumes are summed across all programs at this degree level for the primary reporting window.

Total undergraduate rolls up 47 programs (including undecided inquiry placeholders). In Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 the level generated 149,356 inquiries and 2,629 final enrollments (1.76% inquiry-to-enrollment). Metrics and charts below sum or weight across those programs.

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage). Segment and funnel volumes are summed across all programs at this degree level for the primary reporting window.

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage). Segment and funnel volumes are summed across all undecided inquiry-placeholder programs for the primary reporting window.

Undecided inquiry placeholders combines 9 undergraduate Salesforce programs used when a lead has not selected a degree-specific program. In Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 the group generated 26,205 inquiries and 441 final enrollments (1.68% inquiry-to-enrollment). Sankey and marketing-segment views below aggregate inquiry volume across these placeholders; per-program migration and enrolled-student profile are omitted.

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Business Administration generated 8,715 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 54% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking top-tier (#4 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 266 final enrollments (3.05% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (62.8% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (32.3% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 54%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 63% (below 75% for the level by 12 pts); Navigational 32% (above 22% for the level by 10 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 18% (below 37% for the level by 18 pts); Navigational 54% (above 45% for the level by 9 pts); B2B 27% (above 18% for the level by 9 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 5.8% of total undergraduate inquiries (8,715 of 149,356); 10.1% of level final enrollments (266 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 3.05% (above total undergraduate 1.76% by 1.29 pts); FTG concentration 49% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 29.5% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (48.7% of 1,085 enrolled students; 528 in FTG); 15 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 15 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #1 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 1,085 matriculants, median age 34 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 52.8% female, 8 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (40.6%), in line with the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (48.7%), 15 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 29.5% (9 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (30.3% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 51 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (435 stayed on-program; net +9.3% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

AA in Business generated 10,756 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 30% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking top-tier (#1 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 161 final enrollments (1.50% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (78.5% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (46.9% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 44%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 78% (above 75% for the level by 3 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 7.2% of total undergraduate inquiries (10,756 of 149,356); 6.1% of level final enrollments (161 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.50% (below total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.26 pts); Core concentration 52% vs 38% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 51.8% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (51.8% of 355 enrolled students; 184 in Core); 13 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (38.5%); 13 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (38.5%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (320), BA in Psychology (309), BA in Health Care Administration (263), BA in Applied Behavioral Science (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (210); this program ranks #7 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 355 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 56.2% female, 4 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) (index 93 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is Black (40.9%), 9 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (31.5%); line of business skews to Core (51.7%), 13 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (38.5%); Core share is 51.8% (13 pts above undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (35.2% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 6 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (237 stayed on-program; net +2.0% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Psychology generated 8,571 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 66% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking top-tier (#5 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 146 final enrollments (1.70% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (79.1% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (37.1% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 36%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 79% (above 75% for the level by 4 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 58% (above 37% for the level by 21 pts); Navigational 36% (below 45% for the level by 9 pts); B2B 6% (below 18% for the level by 12 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 5.7% of total undergraduate inquiries (8,571 of 149,356); 5.6% of level final enrollments (146 of 2,629); Core concentration 54% vs 38% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 54.0% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (54.0% of 572 enrolled students; 309 in Core); 16 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (38.5%); 16 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (38.5%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (320), BA in Psychology (309), BA in Health Care Administration (263), BA in Applied Behavioral Science (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (210); this program ranks #2 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 572 matriculants, median age 31 is 2 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 76.0% female, 16 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (45.3%), 6 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to Core (54.0%), 16 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (38.5%); Core share is 54.0% (16 pts above undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (32.0% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 5 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (269 stayed on-program; net +1.6% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Health Care Administration generated 9,437 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 42% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking top-tier (#3 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 140 final enrollments (1.48% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (81.7% of program leads); largest segment Display (46.5% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 44%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 82% (above 75% for the level by 7 pts); Navigational 16% (below 22% for the level by 6 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 33% (below 37% for the level by 4 pts); B2B 24% (above 18% for the level by 5 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 6.3% of total undergraduate inquiries (9,437 of 149,356); 5.3% of level final enrollments (140 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.48% (below total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.28 pts); Core concentration 44% vs 38% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 44.0% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (44.0% of 598 enrolled students; 263 in Core); 6 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (38.5%); 6 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (38.5%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (320), BA in Psychology (309), BA in Health Care Administration (263), BA in Applied Behavioral Science (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (210); this program ranks #3 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 598 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 87.6% female, 27 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (43.3%), 12 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (31.5%); line of business skews to Core (44.0%), 6 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (38.5%); Core share is 44.0% (6 pts above undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (33.8% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 42 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (250 stayed on-program; net +13.2% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BA in Social and Criminal Justice generated 8,395 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 48% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking top-tier (#6 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 112 final enrollments (1.33% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (77.6% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (39.4% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 44%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 50% (above 37% for the level by 13 pts); B2B 6% (below 18% for the level by 12 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 5.6% of total undergraduate inquiries (8,395 of 149,356); 4.3% of level final enrollments (112 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.33% (below total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.43 pts); Core concentration 52% vs 38% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 51.5% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 408 matriculants, median age 29 is 4 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 66.7% female, 6 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (38.7%), 7 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (31.5%); line of business skews to Core (51.4%), 13 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (38.5%); Core share is 51.5% (13 pts above undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Program summary

BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology generated 4,850 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 57% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#9 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 102 final enrollments (2.10% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (71.9% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (30.7% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 45%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 72% (below 75% for the level by 3 pts); B2B 7% (above 3% for the level by 4 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 3.2% of total undergraduate inquiries (4,850 of 149,356); 3.9% of level final enrollments (102 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.10% (above total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.34 pts); FTG concentration 39% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 26.2% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (39.3% of 455 enrolled students; 179 in FTG); 6 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 6 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #3 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 455 matriculants, median age 31 is 2 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 28.8% female, 31 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (34.9%), 3 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (31.5%); line of business skews to FTG (39.3%), 6 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 26.2% (12 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (32.1% of matriculants).

Program summary

BA in Human Resources Management generated 2,648 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 61% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#16 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 90 final enrollments (3.40% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (62.7% of program leads); largest segment Organic (28.9% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 48%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 63% (below 75% for the level by 12 pts); Navigational 31% (above 22% for the level by 9 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 26% (below 37% for the level by 11 pts); B2B 27% (above 18% for the level by 8 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.8% of total undergraduate inquiries (2,648 of 149,356); 3.4% of level final enrollments (90 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 3.40% (above total undergraduate 1.76% by 1.64 pts); Core LOB share 29.1% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (37.8% of 323 enrolled students; 122 in FTG); 4 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 4 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #7 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 323 matriculants, median age 34 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 77.0% female, 17 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (36.8%), 5 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (31.5%); line of business skews to FTG (37.8%), 4 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 29.1% (9 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (31.3% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 5 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (155 stayed on-program; net +2.7% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Homeland Security and Emergency Management generated 9,493 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 21% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking top-tier (#2 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 83 final enrollments (0.87% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (90.1% of program leads); largest segment Display (68.7% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 42%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 90% (above 75% for the level by 15 pts); Navigational 10% (below 22% for the level by 12 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 55% (above 37% for the level by 19 pts); B2B 2% (below 18% for the level by 16 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 6.4% of total undergraduate inquiries (9,493 of 149,356); 3.2% of level final enrollments (83 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 0.87% (below total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.89 pts); Military concentration 56% vs 21% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 26.8% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Military (56.2% of 224 enrolled students; 126 in Military); 35 pts above the total undergraduate share for Military (21.4%); 35 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Military (21.4%); top Military programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (190), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (134), BA in Homeland Security and Emergency Management (126), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (126), BA in Psychology (121); this program ranks #3 for Military.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 224 matriculants, median age 30 is 3 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 26.1% female, 34 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (45.5%), 6 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to Military (56.2%), 35 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Military (21.4%); Core share is 26.8% (12 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (30.8% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 4 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (144 stayed on-program; net +2.4% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Business Leadership generated 3,276 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 63% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#11 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 71 final enrollments (2.17% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (75.1% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (31.4% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 51%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 14% (below 37% for the level by 23 pts); Navigational 51% (above 45% for the level by 6 pts); B2B 35% (above 18% for the level by 17 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 2.2% of total undergraduate inquiries (3,276 of 149,356); 2.7% of level final enrollments (71 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.17% (above total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.41 pts); FTG concentration 54% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 20.3% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (53.9% of 310 enrolled students; 167 in FTG); 20 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 20 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #4 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 310 matriculants, median age 37 is 4 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 48.8% female, 12 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (51.7%), 12 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (53.9%), 20 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 20.3% (18 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (31.9% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 17 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (124 stayed on-program; net +10.7% of applied enrollments).

Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Accounting generated 3,801 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 53% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#10 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 68 final enrollments (1.79% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (73.2% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (31.2% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 37%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 41% (above 37% for the level by 4 pts); Navigational 37% (below 45% for the level by 8 pts); B2B 22% (above 18% for the level by 4 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 2.5% of total undergraduate inquiries (3,801 of 149,356); 2.6% of level final enrollments (68 of 2,629); FTG concentration 44% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 32.5% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (43.9% of 255 enrolled students; 112 in FTG); 10 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 10 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #9 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 255 matriculants, median age 32 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 74.5% female, 14 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (49.0%), 10 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (43.9%), 10 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 32.5% (6 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (31.0% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 5 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (131 stayed on-program; net +3.4% of applied enrollments).

BA in Finance generated 2,047 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 61% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#23 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 67 final enrollments (3.27% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (67.6% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (32.2% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 48%. Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Project Management generated 2,370 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 59% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#19 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 61 final enrollments (2.57% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (72.0% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (29.7% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 54%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 72% (below 75% for the level by 3 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 31% (below 37% for the level by 6 pts); Navigational 54% (above 45% for the level by 9 pts); B2B 15% (below 18% for the level by 4 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.6% of total undergraduate inquiries (2,370 of 149,356); 2.3% of level final enrollments (61 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.57% (above total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.81 pts); FTG concentration 45% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 18.8% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (44.9% of 234 enrolled students; 105 in FTG); 11 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 11 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #10 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 234 matriculants, median age 33 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 33.0% female, 27 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (43.6%), 4 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (44.9%), 11 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 18.8% (20 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (32.5% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 4 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (115 stayed on-program; net -3.1% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Health & Human Services generated 2,743 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 6% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#15 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 55 final enrollments (2.01% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (77.1% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (35.9% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 38%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 40% (above 37% for the level by 3 pts); Navigational 38% (below 45% for the level by 7 pts); B2B 22% (above 18% for the level by 4 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.8% of total undergraduate inquiries (2,743 of 149,356); 2.1% of level final enrollments (55 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.01% (above total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.24 pts); Core concentration 70% vs 38% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 69.5% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (69.5% of 151 enrolled students; 105 in Core); 31 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (38.5%); 31 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (38.5%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (320), BA in Psychology (309), BA in Health Care Administration (263), BA in Applied Behavioral Science (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (210); this program ranks #10 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 151 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 86.8% female, 26 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (49.0%), 18 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (31.5%); line of business skews to Core (69.5%), 31 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (38.5%); Core share is 69.5% (31 pts above undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (45.0% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 9 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (74 stayed on-program; net -9.8% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Operations Management & Analysis generated 1,544 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 47% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#25 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 51 final enrollments (3.30% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (62.3% of program leads); largest segment Organic (31.2% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 49%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 62% (below 75% for the level by 13 pts); Navigational 29% (above 22% for the level by 7 pts); B2B 8% (above 3% for the level by 5 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 14% (below 37% for the level by 23 pts); Navigational 49% (above 45% for the level by 4 pts); B2B 37% (above 18% for the level by 19 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.0% of total undergraduate inquiries (1,544 of 149,356); 1.9% of level final enrollments (51 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 3.30% (above total undergraduate 1.76% by 1.54 pts); FTG concentration 57% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 15.3% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (57.3% of 288 enrolled students; 165 in FTG); 24 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 24 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #5 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 288 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 34.7% female, 26 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (43.8%), 5 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (57.2%), 24 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 15.3% (23 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (28.8% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 5 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (94 stayed on-program; net +4.5% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BS in Information Technology generated 2,880 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 63% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#14 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 46 final enrollments (1.60% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (71.2% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (32.5% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 37%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 71% (below 75% for the level by 4 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 33% (below 37% for the level by 4 pts); Navigational 37% (below 45% for the level by 8 pts); B2B 30% (above 18% for the level by 12 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.9% of total undergraduate inquiries (2,880 of 149,356); 1.7% of level final enrollments (46 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.60% (below total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.16 pts); FTG concentration 46% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 20.7% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (46.4% of 222 enrolled students; 103 in FTG); 13 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 13 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #11 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 222 matriculants, median age 32 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 24.4% female, 36 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (39.6%), in line with the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (46.4%), 13 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 20.7% (18 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (30.2% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 10 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (82 stayed on-program; net +9.3% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BS in Health Information Management generated 2,241 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 21% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#20 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 46 final enrollments (2.05% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (69.0% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (33.9% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 56%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 69% (below 75% for the level by 6 pts); Navigational 25% (above 22% for the level by 4 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 24% (below 37% for the level by 13 pts); Navigational 56% (above 45% for the level by 12 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.5% of total undergraduate inquiries (2,241 of 149,356); 1.7% of level final enrollments (46 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.05% (above total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.29 pts); FTG concentration 45% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 31.1% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (45.4% of 119 enrolled students; 54 in FTG); 12 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 12 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #17 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 119 matriculants, median age 36 is 3 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 91.6% female, 31 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (48.7%), 17 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (31.5%); line of business skews to FTG (45.4%), 12 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 31.1% (7 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (36.1% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 8 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (60 stayed on-program; net -11.1% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BA in Early Childhood Education generated 3,162 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 62% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#12 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 45 final enrollments (1.42% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (75.1% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (26.0% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 49%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 49% (above 37% for the level by 12 pts); Navigational 49% (above 45% for the level by 4 pts); B2B 2% (below 18% for the level by 16 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 2.1% of total undergraduate inquiries (3,162 of 149,356); 1.7% of level final enrollments (45 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.42% (below total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.34 pts); Core concentration 72% vs 38% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 72.0% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (72.0% of 193 enrolled students; 139 in Core); 34 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (38.5%); 34 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (38.5%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (320), BA in Psychology (309), BA in Health Care Administration (263), BA in Applied Behavioral Science (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (210); this program ranks #8 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 193 matriculants, median age 29 is 4 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 90.6% female, 30 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (38.3%), 7 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (31.5%); line of business skews to Core (72.0%), 34 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (38.5%); Core share is 72.0% (34 pts above undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (33.2% of matriculants).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Marketing generated 2,590 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 61% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#17 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 42 final enrollments (1.62% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (71.1% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (37.8% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 43%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 71% (below 75% for the level by 4 pts); Navigational 26% (above 22% for the level by 4 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.7% of total undergraduate inquiries (2,590 of 149,356); 1.6% of level final enrollments (42 of 2,629); FTG concentration 40% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (39.5% of 152 enrolled students; 60 in FTG); 6 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 6 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #16 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 152 matriculants, median age 29 is 4 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 53.3% female, 7 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (43.4%), 4 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (39.5%), 6 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 36.8% (in line with undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (33.6% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 2 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (81 stayed on-program; net +2.2% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BS in Computer Software Technology generated 2,455 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 67% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#18 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 41 final enrollments (1.67% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (74.7% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (35.0% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 46%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 32% (below 37% for the level by 5 pts); B2B 22% (above 18% for the level by 4 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.6% of total undergraduate inquiries (2,455 of 149,356); 1.6% of level final enrollments (41 of 2,629); FTG concentration 49% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 30.0% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (48.8% of 160 enrolled students; 78 in FTG); 15 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 15 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #14 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 160 matriculants, median age 32 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 26.9% female, 33 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (40.6%), in line with the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (48.8%), 15 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 30.0% (8 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (30.0% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 7 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (66 stayed on-program; net +8.0% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BA in Organizational Management generated 986 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 70% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#29 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 40 final enrollments (4.06% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (57.1% of program leads); largest segment Organic (32.1% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 60%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 57% (below 75% for the level by 18 pts); Navigational 38% (above 22% for the level by 16 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 25% (below 37% for the level by 12 pts); Navigational 60% (above 45% for the level by 15 pts); B2B 15% (below 18% for the level by 3 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.7% of total undergraduate inquiries (986 of 149,356); 1.5% of level final enrollments (40 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 4.06% (above total undergraduate 1.76% by 2.30 pts); FTG concentration 54% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 9.5% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (54.5% of 264 enrolled students; 144 in FTG); 21 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 21 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #6 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 264 matriculants, median age 36 is 3 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 40.9% female, 19 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (48.0%), 9 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (54.5%), 21 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 9.5% (29 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (31.8% of matriculants).

Program summary

BA in Supply Chain Management generated 746 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (no prior-period baseline in Apr – Sep 2025), ranking lower volume (#34 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 33 final enrollments (4.42% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (53.1% of program leads); largest segment Organic (38.5% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 52%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 53% (below 75% for the level by 22 pts); Navigational 40% (above 22% for the level by 18 pts); B2B 7% (above 3% for the level by 4 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 24% (below 37% for the level by 13 pts); Navigational 52% (above 45% for the level by 7 pts); B2B 24% (above 18% for the level by 6 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.5% of total undergraduate inquiries (746 of 149,356); 1.3% of level final enrollments (33 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 4.42% (above total undergraduate 1.76% by 2.66 pts); FTG concentration 44% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 12.8% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (43.6% of 39 enrolled students; 17 in FTG); 10 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 10 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #23 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 39 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 33.3% female, 27 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (48.7%), 10 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (43.6%), 10 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 12.8% (26 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (33.3% of matriculants).

Program migration: shows no net 0 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (29 stayed on-program; net +0.0% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

AA in Organizational Management generated 2,059 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 34% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#22 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 32 final enrollments (1.55% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (66.0% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (28.2% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 50%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 66% (below 75% for the level by 9 pts); Navigational 30% (above 22% for the level by 8 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 25% (below 37% for the level by 12 pts); Navigational 50% (above 45% for the level by 5 pts); B2B 25% (above 18% for the level by 7 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.4% of total undergraduate inquiries (2,059 of 149,356); 1.2% of level final enrollments (32 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.55% (below total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.21 pts).

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (41.6% of 101 enrolled students; 42 in Core); 3 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (38.5%); 3 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (38.5%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (320), BA in Psychology (309), BA in Health Care Administration (263), BA in Applied Behavioral Science (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (210); this program ranks #24 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 101 matriculants, median age 37 is 4 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 55.4% female, 5 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) (index 92 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is Black (40.6%), 9 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (31.5%); line of business skews to Core (41.6%), 3 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (38.5%); Core share is 41.6% (3 pts above undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (31.7% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 4 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (64 stayed on-program; net +4.4% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BA in Education Studies generated 1,682 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 59% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#24 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 29 final enrollments (1.72% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Sociology generated 1,519 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 59% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#26 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 28 final enrollments (1.84% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (75.6% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (24.4% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 46%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 46% (above 37% for the level by 10 pts); B2B 7% (below 18% for the level by 11 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.0% of total undergraduate inquiries (1,519 of 149,356); 1.1% of level final enrollments (28 of 2,629); Core concentration 59% vs 38% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 59.0% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (59.0% of 83 enrolled students; 49 in Core); 20 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (38.5%); 20 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (38.5%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (320), BA in Psychology (309), BA in Health Care Administration (263), BA in Applied Behavioral Science (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (210); this program ranks #18 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 83 matriculants, median age 31 is 2 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 79.5% female, 19 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (38.6%), in line with the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to Core (59.0%), 20 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (38.5%); Core share is 59.0% (20 pts above undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (34.9% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 9 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (39 stayed on-program; net -20.9% of applied enrollments).

Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Liberal Arts generated 2,212 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 43% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#21 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 27 final enrollments (1.22% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (76.9% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (35.9% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 30%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 59% (above 37% for the level by 22 pts); Navigational 30% (below 45% for the level by 15 pts); B2B 11% (below 18% for the level by 7 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.5% of total undergraduate inquiries (2,212 of 149,356); 1.0% of level final enrollments (27 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.22% (below total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.54 pts); Core concentration 51% vs 38% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 51.1% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (51.1% of 94 enrolled students; 48 in Core); 13 pts above the total undergraduate share for Core (38.5%); 13 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (38.5%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (320), BA in Psychology (309), BA in Health Care Administration (263), BA in Applied Behavioral Science (229), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (210); this program ranks #19 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 94 matriculants, median age 39 is 6 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 61.7% female, in line with the undergraduate share (60.3%) (index 102 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is White (43.6%), 4 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to Core (51.1%), 13 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (38.5%); Core share is 51.1% (13 pts above undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (27.7% of matriculants).

Program summary

BA in Business Information Systems generated 1,485 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 59% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#27 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 23 final enrollments (1.55% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (73.2% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (30.2% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 30%. Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 30% (below 37% for the level by 6 pts); Navigational 30% (below 45% for the level by 14 pts); B2B 39% (above 18% for the level by 21 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 1.0% of total undergraduate inquiries (1,485 of 149,356); 0.9% of level final enrollments (23 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.55% (below total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.21 pts); FTG concentration 68% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 17.6% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (68.1% of 91 enrolled students; 62 in FTG); 34 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 34 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #15 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 91 matriculants, median age 34 matches the undergraduate enrolled average (33); 46.2% female, 14 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (39.6%), in line with the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (68.1%), 34 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 17.6% (21 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (29.7% of matriculants).

Program migration: shows no net 0 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (44 stayed on-program; net +0.0% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BS in Nursing generated 1,132 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 8% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#28 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 22 final enrollments (1.94% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (44.4% of program leads); largest segment Organic (48.5% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 41%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 44% (below 75% for the level by 31 pts); Navigational 39% (above 22% for the level by 17 pts); B2B 17% (above 3% for the level by 14 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 14% (below 37% for the level by 23 pts); Navigational 41% (below 45% for the level by 4 pts); B2B 46% (above 18% for the level by 27 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.8% of total undergraduate inquiries (1,132 of 149,356); 0.8% of level final enrollments (22 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.94% (above total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.18 pts); FTG concentration 94% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 1.9% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (94.4% of 54 enrolled students; 51 in FTG); 61 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 61 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #18 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 54 matriculants, median age 36 is 3 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 83.3% female, 23 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (55.5%), 16 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (94.4%), 61 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 1.9% (37 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (42.6% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 6 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (31 stayed on-program; net -18.8% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

AA in Military Studies generated 459 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 49% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking lower volume (#36 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 13 final enrollments (2.83% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (65.0% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate (26.5% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 62%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 65% (below 75% for the level by 10 pts); Navigational 35% (above 22% for the level by 13 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Navigational 62% (above 45% for the level by 17 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.3% of total undergraduate inquiries (459 of 149,356); 0.5% of level final enrollments (13 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.83% (above total undergraduate 1.76% by 1.07 pts); Military concentration 76% vs 21% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 19.0% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Military (76.2% of 21 enrolled students; 16 in Military); 55 pts above the total undergraduate share for Military (21.4%); 55 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Military (21.4%); top Military programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (190), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (134), BA in Homeland Security and Emergency Management (126), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (126), BA in Psychology (121); this program ranks #25 for Military.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 21 matriculants, median age 28 is 5 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 23.8% female, 36 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (42.8%), 4 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to Military (76.2%), 55 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Military (21.4%); Core share is 19.0% (20 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (38.1% of matriculants).

Program migration: shows no net 0 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (13 stayed on-program; net +0.0% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BA in Business Economics generated 838 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (up 27833% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking lower volume (#32 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 11 final enrollments (1.31% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (70.4% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (52.5% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 27%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 70% (below 75% for the level by 5 pts); Navigational 26% (above 22% for the level by 4 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 54% (above 37% for the level by 18 pts); Navigational 27% (below 45% for the level by 18 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.6% of total undergraduate inquiries (838 of 149,356); 0.4% of level final enrollments (11 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.31% (below total undergraduate 1.76% by 0.45 pts); FTG concentration 50% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 21.4% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (50.0% of 14 enrolled students; 7 in FTG); 16 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 16 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #32 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 14 matriculants, median age 31 is 2 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 35.7% female, 25 pts below the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (57.2%), 18 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (50.0%), 16 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 21.4% (17 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (35.7% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 1 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (6 stayed on-program; net -12.5% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BA in Communication Studies generated 604 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (no prior-period baseline in Apr – Sep 2025), ranking lower volume (#35 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 11 final enrollments (1.82% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (63.2% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (31.2% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 46%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 63% (below 75% for the level by 12 pts); Navigational 33% (above 22% for the level by 11 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.4% of total undergraduate inquiries (604 of 149,356); 0.4% of level final enrollments (11 of 2,629); FTG concentration 62% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (61.5% of 13 enrolled students; 8 in FTG); 28 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 28 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #30 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 13 matriculants, median age 36 is 3 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 92.3% female, 32 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (38.5%), 7 pts above the undergraduate norm for Black (31.5%); line of business skews to FTG (61.5%), 28 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 38.5% (in line with undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is West (30.8% of matriculants).

Program summary

BA in Child Development generated 941 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 9% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#30 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 7 final enrollments (0.74% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (60.5% of program leads); largest segment Organic (31.0% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 57%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 60% (below 75% for the level by 15 pts); Navigational 35% (above 22% for the level by 14 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 43% (above 37% for the level by 6 pts); Navigational 57% (above 45% for the level by 12 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.6% of total undergraduate inquiries (941 of 149,356); 0.3% of level final enrollments (7 of 2,629); inquiry-to-enrollment 0.74% (below total undergraduate 1.76% by 1.02 pts); Core concentration 67% vs 38% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 67.2% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 61 matriculants, median age 28 is 5 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 96.7% female, 36 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (32.8%), in line with the undergraduate norm for Black (31.5%); line of business skews to Core (67.2%), 29 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Core (38.5%); Core share is 67.2% (29 pts above undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

BA in Instructional Design generated 353 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 71% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking lower volume (#37 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 6 final enrollments (1.70% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (56.8% of program leads); largest segment Organic (32.4% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 83%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 57% (below 75% for the level by 18 pts); Navigational 41% (above 22% for the level by 19 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 17% (below 37% for the level by 20 pts); Navigational 83% (above 45% for the level by 38 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.2% of total undergraduate inquiries (353 of 149,356); 0.2% of level final enrollments (6 of 2,629); FTG concentration 43% vs 34% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 23.3% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (43.3% of 30 enrolled students; 13 in FTG); 10 pts above the total undergraduate share for FTG (33.6%); 10 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (33.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (528), BA in Health Care Administration (206), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (179), BA in Business Leadership (167), BA in Operations Management & Analysis (165); this program ranks #26 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 30 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts above the undergraduate average (33); 63.3% female, 3 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) (index 105 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is White (63.4%), 24 pts above the undergraduate norm for White (39.2%); line of business skews to FTG (43.3%), 10 pts above typical undergraduate mix for FTG (33.6%); Core share is 23.3% (15 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (33.3% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 1 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (12 stayed on-program; net -7.7% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

BA in Social Science generated 296 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (no prior-period baseline in Apr – Sep 2025), ranking lower volume (#38 of 38) among 38 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 5 final enrollments (1.69% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~1.70%).

Lead sources: Paid (67.1% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (37.6% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 20%. Versus total undergraduate lead mix: Paid 67% (below 75% for the level by 8 pts); Navigational 31% (above 22% for the level by 9 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 60% (above 37% for the level by 23 pts); Navigational 20% (below 45% for the level by 25 pts).

Compared to total undergraduate: 0.2% of total undergraduate inquiries (296 of 149,356); 0.2% of level final enrollments (5 of 2,629); Military concentration 67% vs 21% for all total undergraduate matriculations; Core LOB share 33.3% vs 38.5% for total undergraduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Military (66.7% of 3 enrolled students; 2 in Military); 45 pts above the total undergraduate share for Military (21.4%); 45 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Military (21.4%); top Military programs by gross matriculations: BA in Business Administration (190), BS in Cyber & Data Security Technology (134), BA in Homeland Security and Emergency Management (126), BA in Social and Criminal Justice (126), BA in Psychology (121); this program ranks #36 for Military.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 3 matriculants, median age 29 is 4 pts below the undergraduate average (33); 66.7% female, 6 pts above the undergraduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (33.3%), in line with the undergraduate norm for Black (31.5%); line of business skews to Military (66.7%), 45 pts above typical undergraduate mix for Military (21.4%); Core share is 33.3% (5 pts below undergraduate Core at 38.5%). Comparisons use all undergraduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (66.7% of matriculants).

Program migration: shows no net 0 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (5 stayed on-program; net +0.0% of applied enrollments).
