Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

Master of Business Administration generated 4,496 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (flat vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking top-tier (#1 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 244 final enrollments (5.43% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (40.2% of program leads); largest segment Organic (50.2% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 41%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 40% (below 62% for the level by 22 pts); Navigational 38% (above 29% for the level by 9 pts); B2B 22% (above 9% for the level by 13 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 11% (below 23% for the level by 12 pts); Navigational 41% (below 45% for the level by 4 pts); B2B 48% (above 32% for the level by 17 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 17.0% of total graduate inquiries (4,496 of 26,397); 35.0% of level final enrollments (244 of 698); inquiry-to-enrollment 5.43% (above total graduate 2.64% by 2.78 pts); FTG concentration 81% vs 62% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 6.9% vs 17.3% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (81.1% of 962 enrolled students; 780 in FTG); 20 pts above the total graduate share for FTG (61.5%); 19 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (61.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (780), MA in Health Care Administration (133), MA in Organizational Management (99), Master of Information Systems Management (62), MS in Health Informatics and Analytics (53); this program ranks #1 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 962 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts below the graduate average (37); 49.5% female, 11 pts below the graduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (46.6%), 6 pts above the graduate norm for White (40.9%); line of business skews to FTG (81.0%), 19 pts above typical graduate mix for FTG (61.6%); Core share is 6.9% (10 pts below graduate Core at 17.3%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (26.0% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 24 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (433 stayed on-program; net +5.2% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

MA in Psychology generated 3,196 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 21% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking top-tier (#2 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 65 final enrollments (2.03% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (71.1% of program leads); largest segment Non Brand - Search (24.4% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 42%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 71% (above 62% for the level by 9 pts); Navigational 25% (below 29% for the level by 4 pts); B2B 4% (below 9% for the level by 5 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 51% (above 23% for the level by 27 pts); Navigational 42% (below 45% for the level by 4 pts); B2B 8% (below 32% for the level by 24 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 12.1% of total graduate inquiries (3,196 of 26,397); 9.3% of level final enrollments (65 of 698); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.03% (below total graduate 2.64% by 0.61 pts); Core concentration 48% vs 17% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 47.7% vs 17.3% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (47.7% of 153 enrolled students; 73 in Core); 30 pts above the total graduate share for Core (17.3%); 30 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (17.3%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: MA in Psychology (73), Master of Business Administration (66), MA in Human Services (35), MA in Education (30), MA in Health Care Administration (26); this program ranks #1 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 153 matriculants, median age 37 matches the graduate enrolled average (37); 75.0% female, 15 pts above the graduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (39.9%), 14 pts above the graduate norm for Black (26.2%); line of business skews to Core (47.7%), 30 pts above typical graduate mix for Core (17.3%); Core share is 47.7% (30 pts above graduate Core at 17.3%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (33.3% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 2 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (86 stayed on-program; net -2.2% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

MA in Health Care Administration generated 2,887 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 12% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking top-tier (#3 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 57 final enrollments (1.97% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (73.0% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (35.7% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 51%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 73% (above 62% for the level by 11 pts); Navigational 20% (below 29% for the level by 9 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Navigational 51% (above 45% for the level by 6 pts); B2B 25% (below 32% for the level by 7 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 10.9% of total graduate inquiries (2,887 of 26,397); 8.2% of level final enrollments (57 of 698); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.97% (below total graduate 2.64% by 0.67 pts); FTG concentration 71% vs 62% for all total graduate matriculations.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (71.1% of 187 enrolled students; 133 in FTG); 10 pts above the total graduate share for FTG (61.5%); 9 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (61.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (780), MA in Health Care Administration (133), MA in Organizational Management (99), Master of Information Systems Management (62), MS in Health Informatics and Analytics (53); this program ranks #2 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 187 matriculants, median age 40 is 3 pts above the graduate average (37); 81.7% female, 21 pts above the graduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (41.7%), in line with the graduate norm for White (40.9%); line of business skews to FTG (71.2%), 10 pts above typical graduate mix for FTG (61.6%); Core share is 13.9% (3 pts below graduate Core at 17.3%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Northeast (31.6% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 8 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (97 stayed on-program; net +7.3% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

MA in Organizational Management generated 895 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 11% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#12 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 42 final enrollments (4.69% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Navigational (50.1% of program leads); largest segment Organic (48.3% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 50%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 37% (below 62% for the level by 25 pts); Navigational 50% (above 29% for the level by 21 pts); B2B 13% (above 9% for the level by 4 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Navigational 50% (above 45% for the level by 5 pts); B2B 29% (below 32% for the level by 3 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 3.4% of total graduate inquiries (895 of 26,397); 6.0% of level final enrollments (42 of 698); inquiry-to-enrollment 4.69% (above total graduate 2.64% by 2.05 pts); FTG concentration 50% vs 62% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 8.1% vs 17.3% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (50.3% of 197 enrolled students; 99 in FTG); 11 pts below the total graduate share for FTG (61.5%); 11 pts below enrolled-student baseline for FTG (61.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (780), MA in Health Care Administration (133), MA in Organizational Management (99), Master of Information Systems Management (62), MS in Health Informatics and Analytics (53); this program ranks #3 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 197 matriculants, median age 41 is 4 pts above the graduate average (37); 65.3% female, 5 pts above the graduate share (60.3%) (index 108 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is White (40.1%), in line with the graduate norm for White (40.9%); line of business skews to FTG (50.3%), 11 pts below typical graduate mix for FTG (61.6%); Core share is 8.1% (9 pts below graduate Core at 17.3%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (30.5% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 8 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (80 stayed on-program; net +8.6% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

Master of Human Resource Management generated 1,177 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 39% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#8 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 33 final enrollments (2.80% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (51.1% of program leads); largest segment Organic (39.8% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 52%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 51% (below 62% for the level by 11 pts); Navigational 40% (above 29% for the level by 11 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 12% (below 23% for the level by 11 pts); Navigational 52% (above 45% for the level by 6 pts); B2B 36% (above 32% for the level by 5 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 4.5% of total graduate inquiries (1,177 of 26,397); 4.7% of level final enrollments (33 of 698); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.80% (above total graduate 2.64% by 0.16 pts); FTG concentration 46% vs 62% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 22.5% vs 17.3% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (45.9% of 111 enrolled students; 51 in FTG); 16 pts below the total graduate share for FTG (61.5%); 16 pts below enrolled-student baseline for FTG (61.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (780), MA in Health Care Administration (133), MA in Organizational Management (99), Master of Information Systems Management (62), MS in Health Informatics and Analytics (53); this program ranks #6 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 111 matriculants, median age 36 matches the graduate enrolled average (37); 72.5% female, 12 pts above the graduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (36.9%), 4 pts below the graduate norm for White (40.9%); line of business skews to FTG (45.9%), 16 pts below typical graduate mix for FTG (61.6%); Core share is 22.5% (5 pts above graduate Core at 17.3%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (28.8% of matriculants).

Program migration: shows no net 0 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (57 stayed on-program; net +0.0% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

MS in Health Informatics and Analytics generated 1,054 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 10% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#9 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 30 final enrollments (2.85% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (60.3% of program leads); largest segment Organic (32.9% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 47%. Versus total graduate lead mix: B2B 13% (above 9% for the level by 4 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 4.0% of total graduate inquiries (1,054 of 26,397); 4.3% of level final enrollments (30 of 698); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.85% (above total graduate 2.64% by 0.20 pts); FTG concentration 67% vs 62% for all total graduate matriculations.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 79 matriculants, median age 36 matches the graduate enrolled average (37); 72.2% female, 12 pts above the graduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (32.9%), 7 pts above the graduate norm for Black (26.2%); line of business skews to FTG (67.1%), 5 pts above typical graduate mix for FTG (61.6%); Core share is 17.7% (in line with graduate Core at 17.3%). The program recorded 27 final enrollments (1.71% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (71.2% of program leads); largest segment Display (32.5% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 52%. Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

Master of Public Health generated 1,337 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 17% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#6 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 26 final enrollments (1.94% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (69.4% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (28.1% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 42%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 69% (above 62% for the level by 8 pts); Navigational 21% (below 29% for the level by 8 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 19% (below 23% for the level by 4 pts); B2B 38% (above 32% for the level by 7 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 5.1% of total graduate inquiries (1,337 of 26,397); 3.7% of level final enrollments (26 of 698); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.94% (below total graduate 2.64% by 0.70 pts); Core LOB share 23.3% vs 17.3% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (60.0% of 60 enrolled students; 36 in FTG); in line with the total graduate share for FTG (61.5%); in line with enrolled-student baseline for FTG (61.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (780), MA in Health Care Administration (133), MA in Organizational Management (99), Master of Information Systems Management (62), MS in Health Informatics and Analytics (53); this program ranks #9 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 60 matriculants, median age 35 is 2 pts below the graduate average (37); 78.3% female, 18 pts above the graduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (33.3%), 7 pts above the graduate norm for Black (26.2%); line of business skews to FTG (60.1%), in line with typical graduate mix for FTG (61.6%); Core share is 23.3% (6 pts above graduate Core at 17.3%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Northeast (45.0% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 2 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (39 stayed on-program; net -4.9% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

Master of Information Systems Management generated 902 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 7% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#11 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 21 final enrollments (2.33% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (54.3% of program leads); largest segment Organic (38.1% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 52%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 54% (below 62% for the level by 8 pts); B2B 16% (above 9% for the level by 7 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 14% (below 23% for the level by 9 pts); Navigational 52% (above 45% for the level by 7 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 3.4% of total graduate inquiries (902 of 26,397); 3.0% of level final enrollments (21 of 698); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.33% (below total graduate 2.64% by 0.32 pts); FTG concentration 69% vs 62% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 10.0% vs 17.3% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (68.9% of 90 enrolled students; 62 in FTG); 7 pts above the total graduate share for FTG (61.5%); 7 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (61.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (780), MA in Health Care Administration (133), MA in Organizational Management (99), Master of Information Systems Management (62), MS in Health Informatics and Analytics (53); this program ranks #4 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 90 matriculants, median age 38 matches the graduate enrolled average (37); 38.7% female, 22 pts below the graduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (35.6%), 5 pts below the graduate norm for White (40.9%); line of business skews to FTG (68.9%), 7 pts above typical graduate mix for FTG (61.6%); Core share is 10.0% (7 pts below graduate Core at 17.3%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (32.2% of matriculants).

Program migration: gains 6 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (34 stayed on-program; net +14.0% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

MS in Technology Management generated 738 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (flat vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#14 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 18 final enrollments (2.44% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (58.3% of program leads); largest segment Organic (34.1% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 33%. Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

MA in Early Childhood Education Leadership generated 637 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 10% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#16 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 16 final enrollments (2.51% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (54.3% of program leads); largest segment Organic (31.4% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 88%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 54% (below 62% for the level by 8 pts); Navigational 43% (above 29% for the level by 14 pts); B2B 3% (below 9% for the level by 6 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 12% (below 23% for the level by 11 pts); Navigational 88% (above 45% for the level by 42 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 2.4% of total graduate inquiries (637 of 26,397); 2.3% of level final enrollments (16 of 698); Core concentration 37% vs 17% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 36.8% vs 17.3% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (36.8% of 38 enrolled students; 14 in Core); 19 pts above the total graduate share for Core (17.3%); 19 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (17.3%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: MA in Psychology (73), Master of Business Administration (66), MA in Human Services (35), MA in Education (30), MA in Health Care Administration (26); this program ranks #10 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 38 matriculants, median age 39 is 2 pts above the graduate average (37); 84.2% female, 24 pts above the graduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (52.7%), 27 pts above the graduate norm for Black (26.2%); line of business skews to Military (36.8%), 22 pts above typical graduate mix for Military (14.9%); Core share is 36.8% (19 pts above graduate Core at 17.3%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (44.7% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 6 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (26 stayed on-program; net -23.1% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

MA in Education generated 1,279 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 45% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking above median (#7 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 15 final enrollments (1.17% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (68.6% of program leads); largest segment Affiliate - Search (28.8% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 47%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 69% (above 62% for the level by 7 pts); B2B 2% (below 9% for the level by 7 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 40% (above 23% for the level by 17 pts); B2B 13% (below 32% for the level by 18 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 4.8% of total graduate inquiries (1,279 of 26,397); 2.1% of level final enrollments (15 of 698); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.17% (below total graduate 2.64% by 1.47 pts); Core concentration 53% vs 17% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 52.6% vs 17.3% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is Core (52.6% of 57 enrolled students; 30 in Core); 35 pts above the total graduate share for Core (17.3%); 35 pts above enrolled-student baseline for Core (17.3%); top Core programs by gross matriculations: MA in Psychology (73), Master of Business Administration (66), MA in Human Services (35), MA in Education (30), MA in Health Care Administration (26); this program ranks #4 for Core.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 57 matriculants, median age 40 is 3 pts above the graduate average (37); 75.0% female, 15 pts above the graduate share (60.3%) — a notably more female cohort; largest ethnicity group is Black (31.6%), 5 pts above the graduate norm for Black (26.2%); line of business skews to Core (52.6%), 35 pts above typical graduate mix for Core (17.3%); Core share is 52.6% (35 pts above graduate Core at 17.3%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (28.1% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 3 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (31 stayed on-program; net -8.8% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

MS in Finance generated 760 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (flat vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#13 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 15 final enrollments (1.97% of inquiries), near the peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (56.4% of program leads); largest segment Organic (31.1% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 27%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 56% (below 62% for the level by 5 pts); B2B 13% (above 9% for the level by 4 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 13% (below 23% for the level by 10 pts); Navigational 27% (below 45% for the level by 18 pts); B2B 60% (above 32% for the level by 28 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 2.9% of total graduate inquiries (760 of 26,397); 2.1% of level final enrollments (15 of 698); inquiry-to-enrollment 1.97% (below total graduate 2.64% by 0.67 pts); FTG concentration 91% vs 62% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 3.1% vs 17.3% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (90.6% of 32 enrolled students; 29 in FTG); 29 pts above the total graduate share for FTG (61.5%); 29 pts above enrolled-student baseline for FTG (61.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (780), MA in Health Care Administration (133), MA in Organizational Management (99), Master of Information Systems Management (62), MS in Health Informatics and Analytics (53); this program ranks #10 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 32 matriculants, median age 31 is 6 pts below the graduate average (37); 31.2% female, 29 pts below the graduate share (60.3%) — a notably more male cohort; largest ethnicity group is White (40.6%), in line with the graduate norm for White (40.9%); line of business skews to FTG (90.7%), 29 pts above typical graduate mix for FTG (61.6%); Core share is 3.1% (14 pts below graduate Core at 17.3%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southeast (28.1% of matriculants).

Program migration: loses 3 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (18 stayed on-program; net -15.8% of applied enrollments).

Lead inflow & funnel (Sankey)

Marketing segment (initial_marketing_segment1: Display, Affiliate, Organic B2C, Organic B2B, etc.) flows into inquiries, then through the funnel; gray branches are drop-offs. Width = volume. Labels show % of the prior stage so each split (forward + drop-off) sums to 100% (segment inflow = % of segment leads; inquiries onward = % of parent stage).

Program summary

MS in Instructional Design and Technology generated 325 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (down 31% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking lower volume (#18 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 8 final enrollments (2.46% of inquiries), above peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (58.5% of program leads); largest segment Organic (31.4% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 38%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 58% (below 62% for the level by 3 pts); Navigational 34% (above 29% for the level by 5 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 38% (above 23% for the level by 14 pts); Navigational 38% (below 45% for the level by 8 pts); B2B 25% (below 32% for the level by 7 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 1.2% of total graduate inquiries (325 of 26,397); 1.1% of level final enrollments (8 of 698); inquiry-to-enrollment 2.46% (below total graduate 2.64% by 0.18 pts); FTG concentration 47% vs 62% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 41.2% vs 17.3% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (47.1% of 17 enrolled students; 8 in FTG); 14 pts below the total graduate share for FTG (61.5%); 14 pts below enrolled-student baseline for FTG (61.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (780), MA in Health Care Administration (133), MA in Organizational Management (99), Master of Information Systems Management (62), MS in Health Informatics and Analytics (53); this program ranks #14 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 17 matriculants, median age 36 matches the graduate enrolled average (37); 64.7% female, 4 pts above the graduate share (60.3%) (index 107 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is White (70.6%), 30 pts above the graduate norm for White (40.9%); line of business skews to FTG (47.1%), 14 pts below typical graduate mix for FTG (61.6%); Core share is 41.2% (24 pts above graduate Core at 17.3%). Comparisons use all graduate enrolled students in the same window.

Geography: strongest enrollment region is Southwest (29.4% of matriculants).

Program migration: shows no net 0 final enrollments vs same-path inquiry (12 stayed on-program; net +0.0% of applied enrollments).

Program summary

Master of Accountancy generated 725 inquiries in Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 (up 7% vs Apr – Sep 2025), ranking below median (#15 of 22) among 22 programs at the same degree level.

The program recorded 7 final enrollments (0.97% of inquiries), below peer median inquiry-to-enrollment rate (peer median ~2.03%).

Lead sources: Paid (54.5% of program leads); largest segment Organic (26.8% of leads); Navigational share of final enrollments 57%. Versus total graduate lead mix: Paid 54% (below 62% for the level by 7 pts); Navigational 39% (above 29% for the level by 10 pts). Final-enrollment mix differs from level leads: Paid 14% (below 23% for the level by 9 pts); Navigational 57% (above 45% for the level by 12 pts); B2B 29% (below 32% for the level by 3 pts).

Compared to total graduate: 2.7% of total graduate inquiries (725 of 26,397); 1.0% of level final enrollments (7 of 698); inquiry-to-enrollment 0.97% (below total graduate 2.64% by 1.68 pts); FTG concentration 55% vs 62% for all total graduate matriculations; Core LOB share 23.8% vs 17.3% for total graduate.

Enrollment mix (LOB): Dominant matriculation LOB is FTG (54.8% of 42 enrolled students; 23 in FTG); 7 pts below the total graduate share for FTG (61.5%); 7 pts below enrolled-student baseline for FTG (61.6%); top FTG programs by gross matriculations: Master of Business Administration (780), MA in Health Care Administration (133), MA in Organizational Management (99), Master of Information Systems Management (62), MS in Health Informatics and Analytics (53); this program ranks #11 for FTG.

How enrollees differ from average: Among 42 matriculants, median age 37 matches the graduate enrolled average (37); 59.5% female, in line with the graduate share (60.3%) (index 99 vs level baseline); largest ethnicity group is White (47.7%), 7 pts above the graduate norm for White (40.9%); line of business skews to FTG (54.8%), 7 pts below typical graduate mix for FTG (61.6%); Core share is 23.8% (6 pts above graduate Core at 17.3%). Appendix: Methodology & data dictionary

Reference for how metrics in this report are sourced, calculated, and labeled. Generated May 27, 2026. Pull scripts live in the project root; JSON artifacts feed generate_full_report.py.

1. Data sources & time windows

BigQuery — lead funnel

Project / dataset
advertising-data-mart.inquiries
Primary view
vw_lead_extract_details — one row per inquiry; program keyed on program_id at inquiry time.
Date field
inquiry_date (America/New_York calendar days; end dates exclusive).
Primary window (matrix KPIs, widgets, Sankey)
Oct 2025 – Mar 2026 — script: pull_full_data.py, pull_program_detail_widgets.py, pull_sankey_flow.py
Prior window (year-over-year comparison)
Apr – Sep 2025 — same programs, prior 6 months in program_data_full.json (py_* fields).
Monthly detail (all program detail pages)
Apr 2025 – May 2026 — program_data_full.json → monthly keyed by program.
Program migration (all enrolling programs)
Rolling 12 months from current date (NY) — pull_program_migration.py → program_migration.json.

SQL Server — enrolled students & program bridge

Demographics
dbo.StudentRevenueMaster matriculations in the 12-month demographics window (DEMOGRAPHICS_MATRIC_* in report_periods.py), joined via dbo.program_srm_bridge. % Pell among Core LOB; % military funding among Military LOB; transfer credits undergraduate only; dependents excluded.
Enrollment line of business (widgets)
Same SRM matric window; LOB from lineofbusiness on enrolled students.
Account group (matrix column)
account_group from bridge table / data/program_srm_bridge.json (built with scripts/build_program_bridge.py from dbo.vw_program).

Artifact files consumed by the HTML report

  • program_data_full.json — matrix totals, prior period, monthly series
  • program_detail_widgets.json — marketing mix, Paid segment1 breakdown, enrollment LOB
  • program_sankey_flow.json — segment1 inflow + funnel stages
  • program_demographics.json — SRM profile + US regions + degree-level baselines
  • program_migration.json — inquiry ↔ applied program flows (enrolling programs)
  • data/program_srm_bridge.json — program metadata and account groups
  • data/program_alignment.json — college, division, department, and academic leadership by program (from Program Alignment List workbook; scripts/build_program_alignment.py)

2. Metrics & formulas

Lead funnel flags (BigQuery)

  • Leads — COUNT(*) inquiries in window.
  • Apps Started — SUM(is_app_started).
  • Submitted — SUM(is_app_submitted) (monthly table only).
  • Decisions — SUM(is_appin) (application-in / decision stage).
  • Final enrollments — SUM(is_new_enrollment_final); used for matrix Enrl, Sankey funnel, and navigational %.

Summary matrix

  • Prior % change — (current − prior) / prior × 100; shown as ▲/▼/► when change exceeds ±5%; “NEW” if prior is zero.
  • % Core — share of final enrollments with LOB label Core (widget/SRM LOB buckets).
  • % Military — SRM lineofbusiness = Military when demographics exist; else widget LOB bucket Military.
  • % B2B — FTG + TB: SRM Full Tuition Grant + Tuition Benefit (displayed FTG / TB); else same labels in widget LOB.
  • % Navig — navigational_enrollments ÷ new_enrollments × 100 (enrollment mix, not lead mix). Navigational paths: legacy values mapped to marketing rollup Navigational, or null legacy with rollup Brand - Search / Organic.
  • Med Age / % Female — median age_at_matric and female % from SRM (male/female only, renormalized).
  • Net migration — enrollment view: inflow to this applied program minus outflow to other programs (12-month window on program detail pages).
  • Sort order — new_enrollments descending; level total row first, undecided rollup last (undergraduate tab).
  • Green highlight (top 5) — per tab, five highest values among enrolling programs for % Core, % Military, and % B2B (Undecided rollup excluded from ranking).

Detail widgets & charts

  • Leads — marketing rollup — inquiry counts by Paid / Navigational / B2B (resolve_marketing_levels).
  • Enrollments — marketing rollup — same hierarchy on rows with is_new_enrollment_final = 1.
  • Paid details — Paid-only leads and enrollments broken out by initial_marketing_segment1 (segment1).
  • Sankey inflow — segment1 on primary-window inquiries; funnel stages from program_data_full.json funnel block.
  • Monthly Enroll % — new_enrollments ÷ leads per month; App Start % and Decision % use the same lead denominator.
  • 4-month rolling average — mean of leads for months i−3…i; plotted from month 4 onward.
  • Geography index — round(program_region_% ÷ baseline_region_% × 100); baseline = all enrolled students at same degree level (Undergrad or Grad).
  • Demographic “index” callouts — plain-language point difference vs baseline (e.g. “5 pts above”), not the regional index formula.

LOB display labels (report UI)

SRM / widget labelShown as
CoreCore
Full Tuition GrantFTG
Tuition BenefitTB
MilitaryMilitary

3. Marketing segment mappings

Source field: mars_segment_legacy (≈ initial marketing segment on the inquiry). Hierarchy columns match business rollup used in widgets and Sankey. Unmapped legacy + rollup combinations are excluded from segment charts.

mars_segment_legacyMarketing rollupSegment rollupinitial_marketing_segment1
AP - EventsB2BOrganicOrganic B2B
AP - OrganicB2BOrganicOrganic B2B
Agg - Tier 1PaidAffiliateAffiliate
B2B - TPAPaidDisplayDisplay
Call InNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C
Display - FacebookPaidDisplayDisplay
Display - OtherPaidDisplayDisplay
Display - Partner DisplayPaidAffiliate - SearchAffiliate - Search
EP - EventsB2BOrganicOrganic B2B
EP - OrganicB2BOrganicOrganic B2B
Mil - OutreachNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C
OrganicNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C
Paid List - No ConsentNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C
ReferralNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C
Search - GenericPaidNon Brand - SearchNon-Brand Search
Search - TradenameNavigationalBrand - SearchBrand - Search
UnknownPaidDisplayDisplay
oapNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C

When mars_segment_legacy is null, mapping uses BigQuery marketing_segment_rollup:

ConditionMarketing rollupSegment rollupsegment1
null legacy + AffiliatePaidAffiliateAffiliate
null legacy + Affiliate - SearchPaidAffiliate - SearchAffiliate - Search
null legacy + Brand - SearchNavigationalBrand - SearchBrand - Search
null legacy + DisplayPaidDisplayDisplay
null legacy + Non Brand - SearchPaidNon Brand - SearchNon-Brand Search
null legacy + OrganicNavigationalOrganicOrganic B2C

4. US region mapping (enrollment geography)

Student state from SRM → USPS abbreviation → region bucket. Map sketch shows % of program enrollments per region; callout index uses the formula in section 2.

  • Southwest: AZ, CO, NM, NV, OK, TX, UT
  • Southeast: AL, AR, FL, GA, KY, LA, MS, NC, SC, TN, VA, WV
  • West: AK, CA, HI, ID, MT, OR, WA, WY
  • Northeast: CT, DC, DE, MA, MD, ME, NH, NJ, NY, PA, RI, VT
  • Midwest: IA, IL, IN, KS, MI, MN, MO, ND, NE, OH, SD, WI
  • Other:

5. Cursor Agent build context

This report was built iteratively in Cursor using the Agent (Composer) with project rules in AGENTS.md, workspace rules under .cursor/rules/, and Python pull/generate scripts in the repo root. The HTML is static output from generate_full_report.py; data refreshes via BigQuery/SQL pull scripts.

Agent skills (project)

SkillRoleHow it was used
sql-queryPrimaryBigQuery and SQL Server patterns for lead funnel, program metrics, and enrollment pulls (vw_lead_extract_details, SRM).
uagc-brandPrimaryOfficial UAGC colors, typography (Montserrat), and editorial rules applied to report CSS and chart ramps.
ui-ux-pro-maxReferenceDesign-system lookup (styles, palettes, chart guidance); stub in repo pending full package from team.

Additional global Cursor skills (SDK, create-rule, canvas, code-review, etc.) are installed in the IDE but were secondary to the project skills above for this deliverable.

MCP servers

ServerRoleToolsUse in this report
user-bigqueryPrimary dataexecute-query, list-tables, describe-tableLead funnel, Sankey segment mix, marketing widgets, matrix pulls.
user-mssqlPrimary dataexecute_sqlStudentRevenueMaster demographics, LOB, program bridge (CVue codes).
user-gitDeploystatus, diff, commit, log, branchGitHub Pages deploy repo (<code>uagc-program-report</code>).
user-fetchSupportingfetchLanding-page checks and live URL validation.
user-filesystemSupportingread/write file, list directoryArtifact and report file management.
user-memorySupportingknowledge graphWorkspace facts and preferences across sessions.
user-sequential-thinkingSupportingsequentialthinkingStructured reasoning on complex pipeline changes.
user-timeSupportingget_current_time, convert_timePeriod labels and timezone alignment (America/New_York).
user-google-ads-mcpAvailablesearch, list_accessible_customersNot required for this static report; enabled in workspace.
plugin-slack-slackAvailableSlack integrationNot used in report generation.

Token & time summary

Pulled from Cursor usage export usage-events-2026-05-27.csv (2026-05-20 – 2026-05-27, UTC). Figures are agent events only (not IDE autocomplete). Refresh by exporting from the Cursor usage dashboard and saving as data/cursor_usage_events.csv or updating the file in Downloads.

MetricValue
Agent events120
Total tokens88,295,014 (88.3M)
Agent sessions12 (gap > 45 min)
Estimated active time16.1 hours (session span 14.5h + padding)
Work phaseTokens%Est. hoursSessions
Environment & initial build (through May 22)29.6M34%10.79
Report refine & deploy (May 26–27)45.9M52%5.43
Other days in export (e.g. May 20)12.8M14%0.00

Estimated active time = span between first and last agent event per session (>45 min gap starts a new session) plus 8 minutes padding per session. Source: Cursor dashboard usage export (agent events).

Models (by token volume): composer-2.5-fast (51.0M), claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking (37.3M)
